items.title: For more than 4 decades, a mega-music fan recorded over 10,000 concerts. Now many are online
items.summary_auto: Although Aadam Jacobs concert-recording career has come to an end, his love of music and recordings continues to live online, acting as a valuable entity to the music community.

items.title: Curling and golf’s kinship on display as Rock League meets Masters
items.summary_auto: In Scotland, where both golf and curling originated around the 16th century, the sports go hand in hand — and similarities from strategy to sportsmanship to tradition abound.

items.title: MAGA influencers are fed up with Trump
items.summary_auto: A lot's changed since the 2024 U.S. federal election that saw President Donald Trump return to the White House. CBC's Katie Simpson explains how some of Trump's most vocal supporters — right-wing influencers and public figures — are turning their backs on the president amid his recent moves with the Iran war.

items.title: Survivor of intimate partner violence asks P.E.I. MLAs for better protection for victims
items.summary_auto: Clare’s Law allows police officers to provide an individual’s history of abusive behaviour to those who may be at risk. It was named after Clare Wood, a 36-year-old woman in the U.K. who was murdered by a former domestic partner who police knew to be dangerous.

items.title: Former Ontario student pleads guilty in NYC Jewish centre terrorism plot
items.summary_auto: An ISIS supporter who came to Canada on a study permit and planned a mass shooting targeting Jews in New York City has pleaded guilty to a terrorism charge in a U.S. court.

items.title: On-ice expectations were high for the Vancouver Goldeneyes. What went wrong?
items.summary_auto: The Goldeneyes capped off a five-game road trip with a 5-1 loss to Boston on Tuesday. It was the team's fourth loss in five games.

items.title: Class-action lawsuit targets Quebec company accused of producing bogus maple syrup
items.summary_auto: A company accused of producing bogus maple syrup is now the target of a class-action lawsuit request that has been filed in Quebec Superior Court.

items.title: Prosecutors move to subpoena Tiger Woods' prescription drug records after Florida DUI arrest
items.summary_auto: Prosecutors are seeking Tiger Woods' prescription drug records from a pharmacy, a week after his vehicle crashed in Florida and he was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence.

items.title: Stars and producers of The Testaments feel the show's message is relevant 'because of Margaret's vision'
items.summary_auto: The Testaments, an adaptation of Margaret Atwood's book, is releasing its first three episodes on Disney+ today and looking to expand on the chillingly regressive future predicted in The Handmaid's Tale. Its stars and creators talk to CBC News about the message and intent of the series.

items.title: Dean Penney's mother pointed to another suspect 1 day after Jennifer Hillier-Penney disappeared
items.summary_auto: Ruby Penney went to the RCMP on Dec. 2, 2016, and told them about a strange phone call she overheard between Jennifer Hillier-Penney and her cousin Derick Hillier. Ruby Penney is not alive to testify, and Derick Hillier is not alive to defend himself.

items.title: Deputy minister broke rules by hiring unqualified acquaintance: ethics watchdog
items.summary_auto: Deputy minister of national defence Christiane Fox broke conflict of interest rules by hiring an old acquaintance when she was the deputy immigration minister, the federal ethics watchdog has found.

items.title: Despite ceasefire with Iran, Israel's aggressive new attacks on Beirut signal no relief for Lebanon
items.summary_auto: The announcement of a ceasefire between Iran and the United States was met in Lebanon with the war’s bloodiest day, as Israel launched a massive air campaign that its media termed "Operation Eternal Darkness." The Israeli strikes killed and wounded hundreds, as Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu signalled his intent to pursue Hezbollah regardless of international pressure.

items.title: Judge gives 15-year sentence to woman who admitted selling Friends star Matthew Perry ketamine
items.summary_auto: A federal judge on Wednesday handed down a sentence of 15 years in prison to a woman who pleaded guilty to selling Friends star Matthew Perry the ketamine that killed him in 2023.

items.title: Judge tosses Charter challenge filed by woman whose pony was seized by B.C. SPCA
items.summary_auto: Marie-louise Felicia Allen claimed in B.C. Supreme Court that her injured miniature pony was under veterinary care when the B.C. SPCA seized and destroyed it, arguing in her lawsuit that the actions were a violation of her Charter rights.

items.title: Medical expert defends doctor’s assessment of woman before she died of sepsis in Ontario hospital
items.summary_auto: Heather Winterstein didn't show hallmark warning signs of potentially life-threatening sepsis when she went through triage screening the day before she died at the hospital in St. Catharines, Ont., an emergency medical expert told the coroner’s inquest for the 24-year-old.

items.title: Medical expert defends doctor's assessment of Heather Winterstein before she died in St. Catharines hospital
items.summary_auto: Heather Winterstein didn't show hallmark warning signs of potentially life-threatening sepsis when she went through triage screening the day before she died at the hospital in St. Catharines, Ont., an emergency medical expert told the coroner’s inquest for the 24-year-old.

items.title: How northern Ontario researchers are using bacteria-powered tech to extract critical minerals from mine waste
items.summary_auto: A new research facility in Sudbury, Ont., is testing whether bacteria could help solve two major challenges in mining: How to source critical minerals and what to do with the waste left behind. CBC recently toured the 10,000-square-foot pilot facility to speak to researchers and get a first-hand look at how bioleaching works.

items.title: Ontario government's 'special economic zones' law facing constitutional challenge
items.summary_auto: Ontario passed Bill 5, which included the special economic zone provision, last year in a bid to speed up approval and construction of large projects like mines. The provision allows cabinet and the environment minister to suspend any and all provincial and municipal laws within such zones as they see fit.

items.title: With no donation limits, former premier gave N.L. Tories $46,900 ahead of election
items.summary_auto: Former Newfoundland and Labrador Premier Danny Williams donated more than $46,000 to the Progressive Conservatives ahead of last year's election in a province where corporations and individuals can make political contributions as large as they want.

items.title: Aubrey Plaza reportedly expecting her 1st child with Girls alum Chris Abbott
items.summary_auto: The two actors appeared alongside each other in the 2020 film Black Bear and shared the stage in 2023 in the off-Broadway revival of Danny and the Deep Blue Sea.

items.title: Gilgo Beach serial killer admits to killing 8 women in New York state
items.summary_auto: A Manhattan-based architect who led a secret life as a serial killer pleaded guilty on Wednesday to murdering seven women and admitted to killing an eighth in a string of long-unsolved crimes known as the Gilgo Beach killings.

items.title: Lebanon death toll surpasses 200 after Israel launches assault despite U.S.-Iran ceasefire
items.summary_auto: A wave of Israeli airstrikes swept across Lebanon on Wednesday, leaving at least 112 dead and hundreds wounded, according to the Lebanese Health Ministry.

items.title: No sign Iran ceding control of Strait of Hormuz in ceasefire, with logjam expected to continue for weeks
items.summary_auto: Shippers looking to revive the passage of tankers through the Strait of Hormuz were seeking clarity on the logistics on Wednesday, while refiners inquired about new crude loadings, in response to a ceasefire deal between the U.S. ‌and Iran.

items.title: Strikes continue across Gulf countries amid confusion over status of Strait of Hormuz
items.summary_auto: U .S. President Donald Trump is sending an Iran negotiating team, led by Vice-President JD Vance, to Pakistan for talks, the White House told reporters on Wednesday, ‌adding that the first round of negotiations would take place on Saturday.

items.title: Youth charged in Manitoba school attack plot had threatened kids with knife, parents say
items.summary_auto: Multiple Rivers, Man., parents allege 14-year-old charged in a school attack plot had a history of knife threats. They say the community needs better mental health support.

items.title: Oil and fuel prices to remain high throughout the year: Deloitte report
items.summary_auto: The ongoing war between Iran and the U.S. is expected to keep oil prices high for the remainder of the year, including gasoline, diesel and jet fuel prices too.

items.title: $20K worth of alleged stolen booze in a single home: We tracked how N.S. liquor store theft is on the rise
items.summary_auto: After a two-month investigation into significant alcohol thefts from NSLC stores in the Halifax region, RCMP seized more than 450 bottles of hard alcohol worth almost $20,000 from a home. Police even seized a ledger detailing the alcohol being delivered to the home and what had been resold.

items.title: Police uncovered $20K worth of alleged stolen booze in a single home. Inside N.S.'s booze black market
items.summary_auto: After a two-month investigation into significant alcohol thefts from NSLC stores in the Halifax region, RCMP seized more than 450 bottles of hard alcohol worth almost $20,000 from a home. Police even seized a ledger detailing the alcohol being delivered to the home and what had been resold.

items.title: 'The justice system has been allowed to disintegrate': lawyer says N.L. courts are in crisis
items.summary_auto: A first-degree murder trial was delayed last week on Newfoundland’s west coast over a lack of sheriff officers and one lawyer says she wasn’t surprised, given the state of the province’s criminal justice system.

items.title: A workout class sent this St. John's woman to hospital. She's not the only one
items.summary_auto: One woman was admitted with a life-threatening condition called exercise-induced or exertional rhabdomyolysis, rhabdo for short. The illness can happen after an intense workout and is when muscle tissue breaks down, leaking enzymes and proteins into the bloodstream. In high amounts, the proteins can damage the kidneys and lead to an imbalance of electrolytes, which can cause dehydration and heart issues.

items.title: CBC investigation finds numerous Montreal exporters sent stolen vehicles overseas
items.summary_auto: Police raided a Montreal mattress company and found stolen cars. A CBC investigation into the city's car export industry found numerous Montreal businesses have sent stolen cars overseas.

items.title: This teen soccer prodigy is stuck in Quebec with invitation to Real Madrid training camp
items.summary_auto: A 14-year-old aspiring soccer player living in Quebec has been given the opportunity of a lifetime. But his status as an asylum seeker is preventing him from getting on a plane and capitalizing on it.

items.title: 'Every vote counts': Liberals campaign hard for Quebec seat in Monday's federal byelection
items.summary_auto: Federal Liberals are throwing all they can at the Quebec riding of Terrebonne ahead of Monday's byelection, as a 173-seat majority remains a possibility.

items.title: As drones upend tank warfare, Canada's army races to rethink its armour
items.summary_auto: Canada's army is weighing faster timelines for new armoured vehicles while rethinking tanks in light of Ukraine's drone-dominated battlefield. Aging Leopards need replacement, but commanders say technology — including uncrewed systems — will shape decisions, even as other priorities like air defence and long-range strike rank higher.

items.title: From the Gobi Desert to Canada’s North: This Chinese visionary is betting on AI to power this country’s future
items.summary_auto: In China, the energy landscape has reached a historic tipping point. For the first time, its total installed wind and solar capacity has officially surpassed that of coal. It’s a shift driven by massive industrial scale and artificial intelligence — and Chinese energy giants have their sights set on the Canadian wilderness.

items.title: EV giant BYD accused of forced labour violations at European factory
items.summary_auto: As Canada lowers its tariffs and imports more electric vehicles from China, an upcoming report from New York-based labour rights researchers is making new allegations of forced labour practices at the world's bestselling EV manufacturer, BYD.

items.title: The Boys' showrunner says it was a tough call to end the series. 'Politically, I have a lot more to say'
items.summary_auto: As Eric Kripke's superhero satire The Boys releases the first two episodes of its fifth and final season today (with the last dropping on May 20), he's just glad to be able to say the show is ending on his terms.
